title: ON THE PROBABILITY OF GENERATING NILPOTENT SUBGROUPS IN A FINITE GROUP
author_string: JAFARIAN AMIRI, S. M., MADADI, H., ROSTAMI, H.
journal: Bulletin of the Australian Mathematical Society
abstract:
Let $G$ be a finite group. We denote by ${\it
u}(G)$ the probability that two randomly chosen elements of $G$ generate a nilpotent subgroup and by $	ext{Nil}_{G}(x)$ the set of elements $y\in G$ such that $\langle x,yangle$ is a nilpotent subgroup. A group $G$ is called an ${\mathcal{N}}$-group if $	ext{Nil}_{G}(x)$ is a subgroup of $G$ for all $x\in G$. We prove that if $G$ is an ${\mathcal{N}}$-group with ${\it
u}(G)> rac{1}{12}$, then $G$ is soluble. Also, we classify semisimple ${\mathcal{N}}$-groups with ${\it
u}(G)= rac{1}{12}$.
